description: To present synchronized readable text for speech and meaningful sound in a live or recorded event. Captions may identify the speaker and report sound that carries information. 'sitheku' (transcribe) makes a stable textual rendering without requiring it to travel in time with the event.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip lightly to the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th', leave a narrow path for air, and move into true-mid front unrounded 'e'. Close the back of the tongue at the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' before releasing into stressed central 'a'. For final 'si', send unvoiced air through a narrow alveolar groove and raise the tongue into high-front unrounded 'i'.
sound_symbolism: Dental breath, a crisp stressed closure, and a fine final hiss give 'thekasi' three clearly separated beats. Their spacing can recall text arriving in step with an event, though the association is phonesthetic rather than grammatical.
usage_notes: The object is the speech, recording, event, or other material being captioned. Noun use gives captions or captioning according to context. Live or prepared work, completeness, delay, and editing remain available as separate details. Exact format and conformance claims stay with their source.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A gathering can change its communication practice without requiring every participant to use the same sense.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Captions keep speech available when hearing it is not, while speaker identity, laughter, alarms, and other meaningful sounds need not vanish from the record.

semantic_domains.communication: speech and meaningful sound presented in readable form
semantic_domains.creation: timed readable text made for a sounding event
semantic_domains.temporal: text synchronized with a live or recorded event
